item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't you hate it when your mom doesn't appreciate your efforts to save her (non-endangered) life?
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, so scary! Reading the transcript was nightmarish and sad at the same time, which I guess means good job? I do agree that it needs a little something to make it more anomalous, maybe just the fact that the symptoms are so specific in each case? Despite that it's still so horrifying that I have to +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Utterly terrifying. That call transcript shook me to the core.

Take my +1 and never speak to me or my (dead?) mother ever again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, what happens to Dad? Does this only affect men living with mom but without dad? Why are women living with mom immune?

Also:

If at any point the subject's mother loses consciousness, such as falling asleep, they will appear to be deceased through any measurement or observation taken by the subject.

Ok, but if the mother is awake, the log suggests that the subject perceives her as in distress or dying. I think this should be clarified in the description itself.

item.title: Re: I’m having difficulty understanding what makes this anomalous
item.content:encoded: I've made edits to address the first concern. The second I would like to address but it would require more thought than I am able to put in at the moment, but the point is not being ignored.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mental disorders in of themselves shouldn’t be considered anomalous, and doing so can be construed as offensive.

item.title: I’m having difficulty understanding what makes this anomalous
item.content:encoded:
1. It does not immediately show clear to my what makes it anomalous. It could, for example, be another trope that men who are appropriately psychotic will do things like kill their parents. This doesn’t require anomalousness to do.

2. The idea itself is not evocative enough for me to buy its simplicity, mostly because it also affects a very limited demographic and explicitly excludes me from its effect. I have no stakes in this and can never put myself in this, because I’m explicitly ignored in this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is awful
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't see what is anomalous about this rather than a new psychological issue affecting a very small number of people.

If only there was a word that meant "deviation from the norm"

I also don't see anything that suggests that an attempt to understand and contain this (thus justifying the keter rating) has been made.

How do you propose you stop a mental disorder before symptoms even appear?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reluctantly I have to downvote.

I don't see what is anomalous about this rather than a new psychological issue affecting a very small number of people.

I also don't see anything that suggests that an attempt to understand and contain this (thus justifying the keter rating) has been made.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is so simple but so great. It's not the horror that you would suspect, it's more of just oh god please don't let it affect me kinda horror. I love it! all around a great scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so simple yet horrific and tragic.

Reminds me of the Cotard Delusion which makes this anomaly surprisingly believable. Excellent job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah this is pretty messed up, heh. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh no

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why did you make this please for the love of god never make anything like this ever again +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 🦀🦀MOM'S DEAD🦀🦀

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: guess what bois

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh my god I hate it so much +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see this posted! +1 for anomalous matricide

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alternative title: MATRICIDE.

This has been a Roget/Rounderhouse collab. Thank you for reading.

My critters: Rattles, Aismallard, Nico, Roget(?).

More From Roget!
